name: Poulet Au Vinaigre a L'estragon
recipeIngredient: 10 ounces fresh pearl onions (about 2 1/2 cups), blanched in boiling water for 1 minute, then drained and peeled
1 cup chopped and drained canned whole tomatoes
2 tablespoons finely chopped fresh tarragon or 1 teaspoon dried, crumbled, plus fresh tarragon sprigs for garnish (optional)
4 teaspoons arrowroot or 1 1/2 tablespoons cornstarch dissolved in 2 tablespoons cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the onions in a saucepan of boiling salted water and cook for 10 minutes, or until tender. Drain well.
2. In a heavy kettle, heat oil and butter over moderately high heat until hot but not smoking. Brown chicken (patted dry and seasoned with salt and pepper) in batches, transferring to a platter as browned.
3. Add onions to the fat and sauté for 1 minute. Pour off the fat.
4. Add vinegar and wine; boil the liquid, scraping up brown bits until reduced by half.
5. Add broth, tomatoes, dried tarragon (if using), and chicken. Return mixture to a boil, then simmer covered for 20-25 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through.
6. Chicken may be prepared up to this point 1 day in advance: Let cool uncovered, then chill covered. Reheat before proceeding.
7. Transfer chicken and onions with a slotted spoon to a heated platter; keep warm covered with foil.
8. Stir arrowroot or cornstarch mixture; add to simmering liquid with half the chopped fresh tarragon (if using). Simmer sauce stirring for 1 minute.
9. Spoon sauce over chicken; sprinkle remaining fresh tarragon on top and garnish with sprigs.
Serves 6.
